qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via Missing GraphQL Query Depth and Complexity Limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets generic GraphQL query depth and complexity validation gaps in execution and subscription paths, not the advisory's @ExecutionContext() singleton-provider context race.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype Pollution via mergeMiddlewareMaps()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets dangerous-key recursive merging in mergeMiddlewareMaps(), not @ExecutionContext() injection or per-request context isolation under parallel execution.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via Unbounded Session Storage
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Targets Apollo session-map growth in application/apollo.ts, not the advisory's singleton provider @ExecutionContext() context mix-up across concurrent requests.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a CWE-362 race condition in GraphQL Modules where parallel requests that trigger the same service can mix request context when that context is injected through @ExecutionContext() into a singleton provider. The qualifying findings instead cover missing query limits, prototype pollution in middleware merging, and unbounded Apollo session storage. None identifies the advisory's vulnerable ExecutionContext injection path, singleton provider context storage, or parallel-request context mix-up root cause.
evidence: GHSA-53wg-r69p-v3r7 details state that parallel requests using the same service can cause @ExecutionContext()-injected context in a singleton provider to switch between request identifiers.
dos_missing_query_limits/vulnerability_report.json reports lack of GraphQL query depth and complexity validation in application execution/subscription handling.
prototype_pollution_mergeMiddleware/vulnerability_report.json reports recursive merging of middleware maps with dangerous keys in packages/graphql-modules/src/shared/middleware.ts.
No final qualifying report identifies @ExecutionContext(), Scope.Singleton, singleton provider context injection, or the parallel-request context race described by the advisory.
